structured_output.question: What evidence in the last 180 days shows Major League Cricket is planning or updating a league mobile app?
structured_output.answer: Evidence within the last 180 days includes: 1) Jan 30, 2026: Major League Cricket announced a partnership with SecuTix to provide ticketing solutions and a fully integrated ticketing system, signaling a mobile-first approach to tickets (i.e., a mobile app or mobile ticketing component). 2) Related coverage and posts describe a mobile-first ticketing strategy as part of the SecuTix partnership. 3) The official MLC Fan FAQ references a Major League Cricket Mobile Ticketing App, confirming the existence of an app-based ticketing channel. Together, these indicate planning or updating of a mobile app/ticketing experience for MLC.
structured_output.context: MLC has moved to engage a dedicated ticketing platform provider (SecuTix) and publicized a mobile-first ticketing approach in early 2026, which implies development or update of a league mobile app or mobile ticketing system.

structured_output.question: "Major League Cricket" What evidence in the last 180 days shows Major League Cricket is upgrading fan experience or supporter engagement
structured_output.answer: Evidence: On January 27, 2026, the Seattle Orcas announced two Women’s Cricket Brand Ambassadors, Anika Kolan and Jivana Aras, to lead women’s clinics, training sessions, and community outreach, aimed at expanding visibility of women’s cricket and engaging fans across the USA.
structured_output.context: Seattle Orcas press release (via EIN Presswire, republished by The Gardner News) dated Jan 27, 2026, describing ambassador-led initiatives to promote women’s cricket, clinics, and community outreach as a means to engage fans and supporters.
